reviews.title: Nice little bud case
reviews.text: I could imagine a few other ways to utilize this little zippered case, designed for earbuds. But, it works very well for it's intended purpose. Even for some styles that have the 'wrap around' type support, as shown in my pics. It was a little snug, but they fit. The standard buds (either wired or wireless) fit much better. The case itself is nice 'pleather', good zipper and nicely appointed swivel snap to attach it to whatever you want to hang it on. My wife chose the natural leather color to go with one particular purse. She hangs it on the outside of the purse, like a 'bling' effect. Looks great. Good purchase. Five stars.

reviews.title: Just what the doctor ordered
reviews.text: I was looking for a case that would hold my slightly over-sized earbuds without it being too large or too bulky. These fit perfectly in my briefcase and there is plenty of room for my over-sized buds. It's a really  attractive case  for the money. I'm not sure if that is real leather, but who cares as long as they protect  what's inside. The added hook comes in handy since I can attach it to a loop in my briefcase so that it doesn't sink to the bottom and I have to dig for it.

reviews.title: Well made
reviews.text: Very well made.  Anticipate it lasting a very long time.  plenty of room. Use it to hold my headset for cellphone.  Very pleased with product.

reviews.title: Perfect size

title: Skullcandy Grind True Wireless In-Ear Bluetooth Earbuds Compatible with iPhone and Android / Charging Case and Microphone / Great for Gym, Sports, and Gaming, IP55 Water Dust Resistant - Grey/Blue
description: Meet the aptly named true wireless earbud for every part of your daily grind. Say hello to Grind™. Thanks to premium sound, hands-free voice control and a water/sweat-resistant build, these are truly your everyday, do-it-all earbuds. Features Skull-iQ Smart Voice Control technology to control audio, calls, Spotify Tap, and many other features hands-free (Compatible with smartphone devices on Android 10 or newer, and iOS 12 or newer)
features: Note : If the size of the earbud tips does not match the size of your ear canals or the headset is not worn properly in your ears, you may not obtain the correct sound qualities or call performance. Change the earbud tips to ones that fit more snugly in your ear

Supreme Sound - Relax, workout or run listening to your favorite tunes with crystal clear audio, gnarly bass and you'll be listening for up to 40 hours on a single charge. The case holds 31 hours, the buds hold 9 hours of battery
Skull-iQ Technology - Share your tunes with a friend in real time, activate your assistant, customize your earbud controls and trigger your phone camera to take the perfect selfie

Skull-iQ also offers complete hands free, meaning you never need to take off your gloves or let go of the handlebar. Play, pause, adjust volume, take calls, skip tracks, launch Spotify and activate Stay-Aware Mode, all without ever touching your device
Never lost with Tile - With Tile technology, Skullcandy tech makes it super easy to track down either earbud and keep your gadgets safe! Download the Tile app and follow the instructions to activate
Buy With Confidence - 1 Year warranty included with the Grind Fuel, whether you're an adult or child be sure to wear your earbuds in full confidence

reviews.title: Refurb- came coated with previous owner's ear wax - Yuck !  UPDATE !
reviews.text:
non hygenic refurb process disappoints- didn't try the super gross earbuds

UPDATE - I spent the extra $20 for a new set. these have issues. the earbuds turn on by themselves in the case and:
1.) hijack the phone connection
2.) use up all the battery, then charge up, and then run down the battery...so when you go to use the earbuds, the batteries are drained in earbuds and the case

other reviewers write of similar problem

skullcandy chat says they think the case needs to be replaced- I can send them in and wait a few weeks for their warranty repair. but it appears these issues are in the design

or return to amazon within the return period.

yes, that.

reviews.title: Nice sound
reviews.text: These have become my favorite earbuds love the voice activated commands

reviews.title: Stopped Charging
reviews.text: Stopped charging after a month. Return window just expired a few days ago. Now I have to deal with returning through Skullcandy

reviews.title: Comfort!
reviews.text: So the sound quality is good, battery life is good but for me the most important point is they are comfortable. I've been through a LOT of ear buds in search of something that I can wear for several hours without my ears starting to hurt and these are them! Charging case doesn't feel very substantial, but since I don't need to charge them every couple of hours I can live with it.

reviews.title: Interesting app
reviews.text: I've been using these earbuds for several weeks now, and they're as good as any pair I have. And I have several. They connect without issue and have good, clear sound. If you're looking for the intricasies of fine tuning, how good is the base and all that, I'm not the gal for you. However, I think music sounds great, and they're perfect for listening to YouTube vids on my computer and audio books on my phone. The app is interesting. At first I thought I could talk directly to my buds. "Hey, Skullcandy!" (First earbuds I've had with voice control.) But you have to use the app for that feature. Not quite as convenient in my opinion, although perhaps I was expecting too much. The controls are not touch, but require clicking on the individual buds to control them. You can also control them through the app, though. Instructions are clear and easy to understand. I was up and running in minutes. All in all, I think they are excellent and definitely worth the price tag.

title: 6 Packs(12 Pieces) in-Ear Monitor Replacement Memory Foam Tips 3mm Earplugs Earbud Cover for Balanced Armatured Headphone T-100(Medium, Yellow)
description:
Inner diameter: 3mm Outer diameter:12.5mmSize: Medium QTY: 6 pairs
NO EAR FATIGUE.
These foam earphone tips are softer than silicone tip material, Great elasticity and recovery make the tips perfectly fit your ear canal, very comfortable.
BETTER COMFORT
Slow-rebound material, uniquely breath-activated earmuffs activated by body temperature, gives you a new experience without irritation or fatigue.
PREMIUM SOUND.
Soft foam earbud tips provide a perfect seal in the ear canal and pure audio with enhanced bass while eliminate the outside noise.
MORE ISOLATION
With the ear canal properly sealed by the earbud tips, you don't need to increase volume to enjoy music in a noisy environment.
LITTLE USING TIPS
Before wearing earbuds, squeeze the sponge tips with your hands, so they will automatically inflate and perfectly fit your ear canal after wearing. That will create better sound insulation. The memory foam is about 11mm high, the largest diameter is about 12mm, and the inner diameter of the catheter is about 3mm.Made of high-quality, environmentally friendly and non-toxic materials, it has the advantages of softer, slower rebound and more durable. Memory foam is characterized by a slow rebound effect, which can change its own shape with external pressure. From the perspective of earplugs, the ear canal varies from person to person, and the memory foam will change their shape according to the individual's ear canal, making them fit the ear canal best, thus achieving more than ordinary eartips cover good wearing comfort, better noise reduction effect, bring better listening experience. Applicable to the following earplugs: SE series:  SE110/SE112/SE115/SE210/SE215/SE310/SE315/SE420/SE425/SE530/SE535/SE846 E3C/E4C/E5C/E3G/E4G/E500/i3C/i4C/ SC series: SCL3/SCL4/SCL5/
features: 6 Pairs of replacement tips fit on 3mm cavity tube diameter for balanced armatured in-ear monitor.Please measure the outer diameter of your earplug cavity tube before purchasing, and refer to the picture to obtain the appropriate model.
The only earbud tips that adopt separate packaging; each pair is individually packed in its own little baggie; convenient for storage.
Not like other cheap earphone tips, these are high quality memory foam ear bud tips which would not fall apart or disintegrate.
Soft foam earbud tips provide a perfect seal in the ear canal and pure audio with enhanced bass while eliminate the outside noise.
Before wearing, you may need to squeeze the ear tips and put them in your ear and they expand to fit. These are pretty stretchy and stay in place.

reviews.title: Not a quality product
reviews.text: I needed these to fit my Sony earbuds which are always smaller than normal earbuds. I've used Comply in the past and loved them but they're expensive. Should have bought them anyway. These are small plastic tubes inserted into blue memory foam. The tubes don't stretch to wrap around the tip of the earbud the way quality products do and the tubes are apparently not glued to the memory foam as they separate when you're trying to install them.

reviews.title: Hold really well
reviews.text: Really like these foam tips. They hold extremely well in my ears whether I'm hitting hitting the punching bag, jogging or cutting grass.

reviews.title: The quanity you get for the price + the quality is excellent
reviews.text: I like the comfort, quality, and price of these replacement earbuds.  The foam holds up well to use, they never fall off, and they do block out sound around you but not so much you can't hear that someone spoke to you.  The price and quantity is the best on Amazon

reviews.title: Not so great
reviews.text: I bought these as an inexpensive replacement for my ear buds. They took quite a bit of twisting and pushing to get into my ear without feeling like they would fall out. With that, after just a couple days, the outer section broke away from the inner so they no longer fit on the ear bud. I do not recommend purchasing.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement size
reviews.text: Very comfortable, noise isolating, fits my older Phillips SHR 9850 earbuds like new. Like the color because you can tell when they are dirty & need to be replaced.

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: Nice, just don't like having to remove them to charge it
reviews.text: I really like them and my AirPods feel much more secure in my ears.  I can move my head and they don't fall out.  The only thing I don't like is that they won't charge in the charger unless you remove them.

reviews.title: Solves AirPod Issue
reviews.text: I have a love/hate relationship with my AirPods and these are making me love them a LOT more. On their own, my AirPods cause my ears to hurt terribly. Not to mention, they fall out at the slightest movement, like smiling or turning my head. Talk about ANNOYING. These tips cure those ailments. Now I can exercise with my AirPods in and never worry about them falling out. I have tiny ears, and the fit is great with no discomfort at all. I love that the little hook sticks up into the cartilage of my ear to make a really secure fit. The silicon keeps the buds from irritating my ears too. I’m so happy I got these. Now I can actually put my AirPods to good use.

reviews.title: Best for DVDs and Inserts - HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
reviews.text: I've tried a few of these DVD albums, and this is best of the rest. The insert section is roomy enough so you only have to fold along one line and it will slide in comfortably  thus you can see the front and title edge. With Bellagio, you have to double fold, and it creases your insert more than you might want, especially if you think someday you might resell them -- messes with the aesthetics. As far as room, I can slip in 2 inserts, moving one up and under the blank space above and keeping the other low, so able to utilize every slot without feeling like I'm cheating myself by not having both inserts. In addition, for those annoying 3 disc DVDs/BluRay sets, you can slide one of the discs in with the insert, and it will be safe there. It's a sturdy product. I've been using my 240 count for 3 years now, and just bought a 120 in June. The 240 has held up very nicely, and I expect the same of the 120. If you have a large collection you want to transfer, you might want to do some planning ahead of time on how you want group your DVDs, because once you have packed your album, it is difficult to move pages around to reorganize. I had to remove my DVD and inserts. As long as it's not packed, you can manage. The metal clip is very strong and it's a firm hold, but when the slots are full, the bulk of it slides all the pages out because the pages are slick. I didn't have help to hold them in place, and you're going to need it. Leave a few empty in your groupings for new DVDs. The pages themselves are a good quality plastic and soft backing to avoid scratching. It's nicely balanced. I would recommend the 120 more than the 240 because it's easier to carry and store when full but as I said, my 240 has held up very nicely in 3 years. It is currently about 16# full. I don't carry the 240 by the handle. Don't want to push my luck. I can find no damage or weakening in it and every single original page is double packed with CDs  & Inserts, plus, I swiped a few pages from the 120 to put into the 240.  The downside is, if the pages ever do start to rip or tear, you can't find refills -- I've looked.  I have no hesitation in recommending this to anyone wanting to get away from cases and consolidate your DVD collection.  I just haven't found anything else I'm happy with.

reviews.title: These saved my ears! Literally.
reviews.text: My new Bose 700 headphones kept causing an allergic reaction to my ears and the skin all the way around my ears.  Like blisters, redness, swelling and SEVERE itching.  The first time, I wore them for an afternoon and it took 3 weeks for the allergy to be cleared up.  I then washed the cups of the headphones with a soapy wash rag and rinsed well.  The second time I wore them for just under 2 hours and again had a reaction that took 2 weeks to clear up.  I use another brand of headphones daily to watch TV (that I also purchased from amazon) and have never had a reaction before.  I found these covers and they have completely fixed the reaction issue.  I also ordered another brand of ear cup covers off amazon to try but prefer these.  I'll be ordering another so I'll have extras just in case 😉.

But since it wraps over the ear cups, it does not allow the ear cups to rest nicely around your ears.
This means that it affects the headset's noise cancellation a bit.

Also, if you wear it for a prolonged amount of time, it hurts the ears and is less comfortable. I wear glasses and this exacerbates the tension behind my ears.
This is particularly for me with my glasses.

title: FiiO Q5S High Resolution DAC and Headphone Amplifier
features: FiiO Q5S high resolution DAC and headphone amplifier

reviews.title: The little DAC with big magic
reviews.text:
This gadget is simply ahead of its time. For audio enthusiasts (like me) who dare to explore how the right DAC can elevate the experience of music listening to a delightful level, this DAC won't disappoint. It brings out the best in headphones (reasonably decent ones), which in my case were a Sennheiser HD 600 and a budget triple-driver IEM. Also, it breathed life into the premium speakers of my old car--simply unbelievable; I never heard them sound so good (the DAC's line-out was fed into the car's aux-in). My tracks came from Amazon HD Music.

As a gadget aficionado in general, I find this DAC well-designed, functionality- and aesthetics-wise, and with a solid build and classy finishing that clearly distinguish it from the other DACs in this price range. It's one of the best gadgets I bought during recent years.

reviews.title: No quality control.
reviews.text: Ordered a “like new” used item. Description said it had a 4.4mm headphone jack.  The box was a relatively new, but item inside was nothing like described.

reviews.title: Much better than expected...Updated!!! MQA use...
reviews.text:
02/16/21
I've been using the Q5s TC for some weeks now mainly with Apple Music and sometimes the SIRIUSXM app for iOS on my iPhone 12 Pro. Just a days ago I decided to check out MQA music on TIDAL. I have multiple headphones and settled on Shure SRH1540 and SRH1840. I have listed a number of headphones in other reviews so I won't go there. Anyway, WOW!!!, what a revelation. Listening to MQA's through TIDAL is very revealing and at one point on some recordings I had to say to myself, "there were anymore detail and layering I would be able to taste and smell it". I can also easily hear what I can assume is in some cases budgets, engineering and anything else that might affect the way a recording sounds.

Now from my understanding the Q5s does not unpack the MQA music file but it appears. it's definitely seeing and recognizing the file, if not totally able to unpack it. I say this because the indicator on the front glows a solid yellow which according to FiiO is AptX HD, don't know but it makes a difference. Talking about spacial clues? all over the place. But of course it depends on the recording. I can say this, listening to all my old new music, from Aretha to Soundgarden and more is so much fun and enjoyable.

I'm a ex-audiophile that actually love music more than the equipment (most of the time). The Apple AirPods Max reviews and rages made me revisit headphone collection. After selling 3 and decommissioning 1 I ended up with my mostly hated (not by me, I love the way sound) Sennheiser HD700. Measurements and other people's bias' aside I decided to put a dac/amp in my setup for my iPhone 12 Pro. The Q5s plugs right into my iPhone via the supplied connector with no issues at all. The bluetooth works great as well. I prefer direct connection over using BT mainly because I want to as close to the true signal as possible. AAC sounds great through this dac/amp combo. I can't hear a real difference the 2. The Q5s is a solid and professional quality dac/amp combo. It does get a little warm while running but nothing that concerns me. I'm also using the Shanling UP4 which is to my ears good too and serves another purpose in modest collection. The Shanling is not capable of plugging directly into the iPhone but does a really decent job with BT. I prefer the direct connection ability of the Q5s. Of course like I mention the build quality is in a different league as it should be due to the price difference between the 2.

reviews.title: Ain’t loud enough...
reviews.text: It sounded low volume, muffled & cluttered to me. Didn’t sound like an *amp* with dual chips. BT wasn’t strong enough either.

reviews.title: They fixed all of my complaints with the Q5
reviews.text:
1) One USB port
2) Volume knob is also the power-on, as found on the Q1
3) Nice input selector on the right with some labeled LEDs that also show battery charging level
4) Fewer buttons
5) Bluetooth 5.0 can do 24-bit/96khz, so need cable only for charging once Apple supports aptX HD (audiophile-only issue)

Before going any further, I upgraded the firmware from 0.04 to the latest (0.39). The frequency response with 0.39 seems a lot smoother.

The Q5s sounds different from Q5. It's a clearer, more detailed sound. I also like that the bass level is a little higher with the OFF setting. I use ATH-M50x headphones.

So, FiiO, if you are listening, here are my new suggestions:

a) Replace the microUSB port with USB-C. It's a much more reliable connection. microUSB gets loose over time.
b) Have a bass switch or knob that allows for more bass settings. Actually, the EQ feature on the FiiO app is probably a better way to go, assuming it is remembered when not using the app. I have not tried it yet. If that's the case, why not get rid of the base boost button altogether?
c) Have different sleep timeouts for charging vs. not charging. Add 1 hour, 2 hour, and Never as possibilities to each.

Non-FiiO problem: macOS and iOS do not support Bluetooth aptX HD or LDAC, so I will need to wait for 24-bit/96khz over BT 5.0. I submitted a bug to Apple. I hope others will do the same.

reviews.title: Just What I Wanted
reviews.text: I wanted to replace my old clunker of a boom box.  I needed a small CD player with_ a speaker so I wouldn't have to wear ear buds.  That's what I found here. At first, I wondered about the sound, but considering how small it is, the sound is fine.  I'm glad to have easier access to my CD collection.

reviews.title: Great Bluetooth CD player
reviews.text:
I have a few CDs that I have kept despite mostly using streaming services. Music not available online. This device allows me to hear them again.
Connected very easy to my Bluetooth tailgate speaker.

Don't plan on using the built in speaker as I have several better speakers that I can connect to with cable or Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Sound Quality not the best on its own speakers
reviews.text:
I found using the Aux cable along with my Bluetooth headphones the sound quality is much better.
The headphones that came with unit wasn't that good.

reviews.title: Very Good Portable CD Player With Plenty of Options
reviews.text:
With the onset of Pandora, satellite radio and many other such options, I found I wasn’t playing my CDs anymore. In fact, I couldn’t find my CD player! And the high-end system in my truck doesn’t even have a CD option. So when I saw this I figured I give it a try. I like that it’s portable and plays without skipping even when bumped, running with it, or the dog knocking it off of the table. It can be used with my several different sets of earphones. It also plugs into my truck stereo without a problem.

I also wanted it to play through its own speaker while in my crafts room. This is where the 5th star gets removed. When turning it up nearly all the way the speaker starts to buzz on certain notes. It isn’t loud at all on its own either so using it without auxiliary speakers is not good if more than about 8 feet from it. However, I am playing my CDs which I have not heard in years, and that makes me very happy.

reviews.title: Fun and good
reviews.text:
My 13 yo son is on the vintage wave, so he has been using this and the cassette one. He is happy!
Very portable, small size.
The sound is ok.
Has been using it non stop and still working good.
Would be a fun gift for someone who likes old school (on a fun way, not like a collector)
We like it.

reviews.title: Difficult to connect. Oddly LOW VOLUME and INTERFERES with apps like VIBER and DUO. Easy to loose.
reviews.text:
Where do I start:::
Connecting, if you want to use it with multiple devices, you can just forget it (if connected to one device, they will not allow you to connect to another device until you actually delete the connection from the current device even if it's off, did not have this issue with Apple Pods).
SOUND LEVEL is oddly LOW. Seriously, even at max setting does not even come close to even cheap plug-in buds, so can't use them in any loud place. Noise canceling does not help with that.
If used with call apps like Viber or Duo you'll be missing every other word. Don't know how or why, but connection signal goes to crap while these are on. As soon as I switch to plug-in buds, everything works just fine.
TOO DAMN SMALL. Sure it's convenient for the ear, but trying to handle them like putting in or taking out, better be sure to do it somewhere safe in case you drop them (not outside for sure). It would be better if they had over the year clips or something.
COLLECTS all your EAR-WAX. Yes, these things go in so deep that it just gets absolutely gross (never had this problem with other buds and I clean my ears every day)

reviews.title: Yellowing?
reviews.text: I’ve had these for less than a month and really love them. Easy to put on and stay put. Today I noticed that one of covers is starting to yellow. I don’t typically keep my headphones anywhere except on my head or in the case, so i have not gotten them dirty to cause this. Anyone else?
